title: T T T Tetramorph
wikitext:
[]: T-T-T-Tetramorph!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 17, 2007 9:58 pm Alright, I was browsing information about different mythological creatures, and found information about the Tetramorph , a Biblical creature described by Ezekiel. Compare the image there: to this: They both have wings at very similar angles, and they both have eyes all over them. Plus both have big circles in the center. Yeah, so... I think it's pretty evident that picture from the OP was patterned after the Tetramorph. I find it kind of odd the Tetramorph has never been mentioned on this forum. Or was there another similar Biblical image that looks even more like that from the OP? EDIT: Apparently the old commentary mentions a Biblical seraphim that is described similarly to the Tetramorph, but states that the comparison is "tenuous at best". I think this picture, though, makes it much less tenuous.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Wed Oct 17, 2007 10:02 pm no that has 6 pairs of wings I think it's supposed to evoke a Seraph

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 17, 2007 10:52 pm V wrote: no that has 6 pairs of wings I think it's supposed to evoke a Seraph

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Oct 17, 2007 11:17 pm Huh. Interesting find.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 17, 2007 11:28 pm ya, but not surprising. Eva has to get its Religious symbols somehwere

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:36 am Im thinking Seraph

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:39 am Cool find. If it is a tetramorph, is there any relevance to the series, though?

[]: Re: T-T-T-Tetramorph!             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:53 am I did a quick search to find out what the Tetramorph actually is, and it turns out to be essentially the same thing as the signs of the Four Evangelists (the image might look familiar...), albeit combined into a single form (implied by the term "tetramorph"). Good find, NAveryW -- this solves the long-standing mystery of what the Sammael image was modeled after. The Four Evangelists are not angels themselves (so neither would be the Tetramorph), although they're depicted with related imagery (wings, etc.).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:56 am First seen in Ezekiel 1:4-9 http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ezekiel%201:4-9;&version=9;

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Oct 20, 2007 12:51 pm I thought it was Satan... you know, before he was "cast down", and that it had all to do with "humanity's free will", because in order to survive, they had to "sin"... well that will teach me from listening to a [URL=http://stephsthoughts.brainage.org/downloads/eva-interpretation.pdf]Eva-Kabbalism essay[/URL] that I didn't fully trust in the first place... or did I hear that on the Trivial Being boards? Hmm... 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Oct 20, 2007 1:15 pm Alaska Slim wrote: I thought it was Satan...

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 20, 2007 1:46 pm When combined, do they have a rocket punch or a super wave cannon?

[]: Re: T-T-T-Tetramorph!             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Oct 20, 2007 3:12 pm NAveryW wrote: a Biblical creature described by Ezekiel.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 20, 2007 4:20 pm That image can't be right. I don't see heads of any animals on that alien craft! EDIT: Oh, and thanks, Reichu. Does this mean that we're still going to call it Sammael, though? I've always thought it was kind of a jump to decide that it's Sammael simply because of the number of wings. But that kind of attention to detail does often exist, so I wouldn't say it's too much of a jump in any case.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Oct 20, 2007 6:14 pm NAveryW wrote: That image can't be right. I don't see heads of any animals on that alien craft!

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sat Oct 20, 2007 7:25 pm NAveryW wrote: That image can't be right. I don't see heads of any animals on that alien craft!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Oct 20, 2007 8:36 pm Barinax wrote: Well the top sort of looks like a penguin...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 20, 2007 9:33 pm Lucifer actually technically isn't even the devil.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:06 pm The image is certainly similar enough to the "Angel Logo" image that we can say that something like it inspired the logo. Whether the creature known as the Tetramorph itself was the actual base image is I think a matter needed further confirmation. It certainly looks likely that early christian imagery was the original source of the logo image. Am I mistaken, or is this Greek Orthodox imagery?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Oct 20, 2007 10:40 pm Does anyone have that pic of Sammael from a church ceiling in eastern Europe that Zuggy (IIRC) posted a while back?
